channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Karen Read's second trial heads into weekend without full jury seated
item.description: Ten of 16 jurors have been selected for Karen Read's second murder trial in the death of Boston cop John O'Keefe as the court breaks for its first weekend.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Senate GOP approves framework for Trump's tax breaks and spending cuts after late-night session
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Senate Republicans plugged away overnight and into early Saturday morning to approve their multitrillion-dollar tax breaks and spending cuts framework, hurtling past hardened Democratic opposition toward what President Donald Trump calls the “big, beautiful bill” that's central to
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, LEAH ASKARINAM and KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Singer Rara Sekar draws inspiration from nature, encourages people to return to simple living
item.description: OXFORD, England (AP) — Rara Sekar closed her eyes in meditation after performing a song that speaks of rays of light that cut through the fog as one political prisoner faced death more than six decades ago.
item.dc:creator: RODNEY MUHUMUZA and VICKRAM SOMBU, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: More torrential rain and flash flooding expected in heavily waterlogged South and Midwest
item.description: HOPKINSVILLE, Ky. (AP) — Another round of torrential rain and flash flooding was expected to hit Saturday in parts of the South and Midwest already heavily waterlogged by days of severe storms that in some cases spawned deadly tornadoes. Round after round of heavy rains have pounded the central U.S.
item.dc:creator: GEORGE WALKER IV and BRUCE SCHREINER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Things to know about the US Mine Safety and Health Administration and the coal industry
item.description: CHARLESTON, W.Va. (AP) — The U.S. Mine Safety and Health Administration is among the federal agencies selected for spending cuts by the Department of Government Efficiency. Nearly three dozen MSHA offices would have their leases terminated if the plans come to fruition.
item.dc:creator: JOHN RABY and LEAH WILLINGHAM, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Prosecutors seek 7 years in prison for disgraced ex-US Rep. George Santos in federal fraud case
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Prosecutors are seeking more than seven years in prison for disgraced former U.S. Rep. George Santos after he pleaded guilty to federal fraud and identity theft charges. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: PHILIP MARCELO, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: YouTube daredevil arrested by Indian authorities after visiting remote island where US missionary was killed
item.description: A YouTube influencer visiting a remote Indian island, where he allegedly tried to make illegal contact with indigenous people, has been arrested, according to global reports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Las Vegas Sphere launching 'experience' around classic film
item.description: Sphere, the famed immersive Las Vegas entertainment arena, is set to debut two new "Sphere Experiences" later this year — and one is based on a beloved classic motion picture.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KRON4

item.title: Farmers fear tariffs could cost them one of their biggest markets in China
item.description: OMAHA, Neb. (AP) — Heading into this year, most U.S. farmers were hoping to break even or maybe record a small profit if they could find a way to limit their sky-high costs.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Actor Glen Powell launches organic condiments line
item.description: Actor and producer Glen Powell has launched Smash Kitchen, a line of organic condiments that includes non-GMO pantry essentials with remixed flavors, now exclusively available nationwide at Walmart stores and Walmart.com.
item.dc:creator: Erica Pauda, KRON4

item.title: Trump administration rolls back forest protections in bid to ramp up logging
item.description: President Donald Trump's administration has rolled back environmental protections around future logging projects on more than half of U.S. national forests under an emergency designation issued Friday that cites the dangers of wildfires.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Brown, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: 100-year-old tortoise gives birth to critically endangered hatchlings at Philly Zoo
item.description: For the first time in over 150 years, the Philadelphia Zoo has new additions to the Western Santa Cruz Galapagos tortoise family.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Yakubovsky, Fox 5

item.title: Baby boomers are buying more homes than millennials
item.description: Older buyers — who are more likely to purchase homes with cash — have been less affected by elevated mortgage rates.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, NewsNation

item.title: US must return Maryland father mistakenly deported to El Salvador prison, judge rules
item.description: A federal judge on Friday ordered the Trump administration to arrange for the return of a Maryland man to the United States after he was mistakenly deported to a notorious El Salvador prison.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump to give TikTok another 75-day extension
item.description: President Trump said Friday that he plans to give TikTok another 75-day extension as his administration nears a deal to avert a ban on the popular social media platform.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fyre Festival II not happening at 2nd location, Mexican officials say
item.description: A major issue looms for the planned Fyre Festival II. A representative from Playa del Carmen's City Hall stated that the music festival is not scheduled to take place there. Just last week, Bill McFarland revealed that the festival was moved from Isla Mujeres to Playa del Carmen.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, KTLA 5

item.title: Rachel Morin’s alleged illegal killer left slain jogger’s body along 150-foot blood trail: prosecutors
item.description: Opening statements began in the trial of an illegal immigrant charged with murdering Rachel Morin, a Maryland mother of five who was killed while jogging on a hiking trail.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Comedian Russell Brand charged with rape and sexual assault in UK
item.description: British police on Friday charged Russell Brand with rape and sexual assault following an 18-month investigation sparked when four women alleged they had been assaulted by the controversial comedian.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump ordered to pay legal fees in ‘Steele Dossier’ lawsuit
item.description: A judge in the United Kingdom has ordered President Donald Trump to pay more than $820,000 in legal fees to the company representing former British spy Christopher Steele after he unsuccessfully sued over a dossier.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KRON4

item.title: HHS staff working on childhood lead exposure and cancer clusters fired
item.description: Staff members who fought childhood lead exposure and those who worked on cancer clusters were among those who were fired from the Department of Health and Human Services (HHS), a now former employee told The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Senators introduce bipartisan bill to give Congress more power over tariffs
item.description: If passed, the Trade Review Act of 2025 would require the president to notify lawmakers of an imposition or increase in tariffs within 48 hours.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, NewsNation

item.title: How the White House calculated reciprocal tariffs
item.description: President Trump triumphantly held up a poster board in the Rose Garden on Wednesday showing the reciprocal tariffs he plans to impose on nearly every country in the world.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Fencer refuses to compete against transgender opponent
item.description: Turner told NewsNation she didn’t “want to let people know that I'm OK with this and pretend like nothing's happening.
item.dc:creator: Rich McHugh, NewsNation

item.title: What you do before and during a tornado could mean the difference between life and death
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — With new innovations and technology, scientists have learned from major tornado outbreaks over the years how to improve safety guidance. During tornado season, forecasters try to get the word out about staying safe during a storm — but also planning for it ahead of time.
item.dc:creator: JEFF MARTIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Teen stabbed at track meet, dies in his twin brother's arms, father says
item.description: A 16-year-old is dead, and the suspect is in custody after an altercation between two students escalated to a stabbing at a track meet in Frisco, the Frisco Police Department said in a news release Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Erica Pauda, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Deadly storms slash through the South and Midwest
item.description: The coming days were also forecast to bring the risk of potentially deadly flash flooding to the South and Midwest as severe thunderstorms blowing eastward become supercharged.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Yankees OF Brett Gardner's son died of carbon monoxide poisoning, had 'layer' on organs, officials say
item.description: Randall Zúñiga, director of the Judicial Investigation Agency, said they tested the 14-year-old Miller Gardner for carboxyhemoglobin, a compound generated when carbon monoxide binds to hemoglobin in the blood.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, WGN 9

item.title: Juice sold from New York to wineries in 12 states recalled over potentially deadly toxin
item.description: A wine juice wholesaler in New York has recalled a pumpkin wine juice sold to wineries in a dozen states because it contains a toxin that could be deadly.
item.dc:creator: Madalyn Buursma, Fox 5

item.title: Tariffs expected to hike up costs of Toyota trucks made in Baja
item.description: "I expect prices to go up probably in a few weeks," he said. "It will also be more expensive for people to service and repair their cars since many parts come from Mexico and China.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, KTLA 5

item.title: 1 person dead as violent storms, including tornadoes, wallop Midwest and South
item.description: Tornadoes and violent storms struck parts of the South and Midwest on Wednesday, killing at least one person, knocking down power lines and trees, ripping roofs off homes and shooting debris thousands of feet into the air.
item.dc:creator: JEFF MARTIN and HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump announces sweeping 'reciprocal' tariffs
item.description: President Donald Trump announced plans for sweeping tariffs Wednesday, saying “our country has been looted, pillaged, raped, plundered” by other nations.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Musk could be headed for a Washington exit after turbulent times at DOGE
item.description: President Trump has said that DOGE will end at some point and Musk will return to running his companies.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Beloved Anchorage pet reindeer euthanized after someone tampered with pen, possibly poisoned him
item.description: ANCHORAGE, Alaska (AP) — An iconic pet reindeer beloved by many in Alaska's largest city has been euthanized, just weeks after someone tampered with his cage and possibly poisoned him, his caretaker said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: MARK THIESSEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Potential Trump tariff plan could cost households thousands: new analysis
item.description: A new analysis from The Budget Lab at Yale shows how much a broad tariff plan could cost U.S. households.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, WFLA, NBC

item.title: New documents describe physical condition of man allegedly held captive for decades
item.description: An investigator said the man's skin appeared "to be graying," and that his "physical condition resembles one who would be at a point at the end of their life.
item.dc:creator: Jenn Brink, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Scientists fear impact of National Weather Service cuts
item.description: As the summer hurricane season looms, so too do cuts to the federal workforce, including the National Weather Service.
item.dc:creator: BJ Lutz, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Wild weather blamed for deaths of 3 kids in Michigan and a man in an Amish buggy in Indiana
item.description: KALAMAZOO, Mich. (AP) — A tree fell on a van in Michigan, killing three children, during a fierce weekend storm that swept the region and contributed to the death of an 84-year-old man in an Amish buggy in Indiana, authorities said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4
